title: Properties of Windows Application Shells
text: Properties of Windows Application Shells For Windows applications, you can define a background image, title bar, and other properties of the application window. Presentation Properties The following properties can be set using the Properties Inspector in the Application Shell Editor. They cannot be changed at runtime using ProcScript. Title Library Menu Bar Pop-up Menu Panel Session Panel Panel Position Window and Image Properties To edit window and background image properties, click the More icon ( ) in the Window/App property. Table 1. Window Properties Property Technical Property Name Background Color (BackColor) BackColor Caption Caption Close (CanClose) [APS] CanClose Dialog Dialog Iconize (CanIconize) CanIconize Maximize CanZoom Maximized Forms MaximizedForms Message Line MessageLine Resizable (CanResize) CanResize Setup Setup System Menu (SysMenu) SysMenu The following properties apply to a background image, if specified. To edit them, click the Image tab in the Application Shell properties dialog. Table 2. Image Properties Property Technical Property Name Background Image (BackImage) BackImage Horizontal Alignment (Halign) HAlign Vertical Alignment ( Valign) VAlign Horizontal Scaling ( Hscale) Hscale Vertical Scaling ( Vscale) VScale Preserve Aspect Ratio ( PreserveAspect) PreserveAspect Default values for BackColor, MessageLine, and image properties can be set using the Shell setting in the usys.ini file. Related Links Shell Sets the default window properties of the application shell. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
